heading: 9.Instruction Set (part 81)
content:
| .ctype / .dtype | Fragment | Elements (low to high) |
  | --- | --- | --- |
  | `.s32` | A vector expression containing four `.s32` registers, containing four `.s32` elements from the matrix C (or D). | c0, c1, c2, c3 |

  The layout of the fragments held by different threads is shown in [Figure 99](#mma-168128-c).

  



[Figure: mma-168128-C]
MMA .m16n8k128 Fragment Layout for Accumulator Matrix C/D with .s32 Type

OVERVIEW
Matrix dimensions: M=16, N=8, K=128
Matrix role: Accumulator (C/D)
Data type: .s32
Each .s32 element occupies one full 32-bit register (no packing; one element per register)
Each thread holds 4 registers total: c0, c1, c2, c3

GRID STRUCTURE
The table has rows labeled R (matrix rows 0-15) and columns labeled C (matrix columns 0-7).
Columns are grouped in pairs: {0,1}, {2,3}, {4,5}, {6,7}.

TABLE LAYOUT (R\C = matrix row \ column group)

R\C     Cols 0-1          Cols 2-3          Cols 4-5          Cols 6-7
0       T0:  {c0, c1}     T1:  {c0, c1}     T2:  {c0, c1}     T3:  {c0, c1}
1       T4:  {c0, c1}     T5:  {c0, c1}     T6:  {c0, c1}     T7:  {c0, c1}
2       [continuation arrow indicating pattern continues through rows 2-6]
...
7       T28: {c0, c1}     T29: {c0, c1}     T30: {c0, c1}     T31: {c0, c1}
8       T0:  {c2, c3}     T1:  {c2, c3}     T2:  {c2, c3}     T3:  {c2, c3}
9       T4:  {c2, c3}     T5:  {c2, c3}     T6:  {c2, c3}     T7:  {c2, c3}
10      [continuation arrow indicating pattern continues through rows 10-14]
...
15      T28: {c2, c3}     T29: {c2, c3}     T30: {c2, c3}     T31: {c2, c3}

DETAILED THREAD-TO-ELEMENT MAPPING

Matrix rows 0-7 (upper half), registers c0 and c1:
  Row 0: T0 holds cols 0-1, T1 holds cols 2-3, T2 holds cols 4-5, T3 holds cols 6-7
  Row 1: T4 holds cols 0-1, T5 holds cols 2-3, T6 holds cols 4-5, T7 holds cols 6-7
  Row 2: T8 holds cols 0-1, T9 holds cols 2-3, T10 holds cols 4-5, T11 holds cols 6-7
  Row 3: T12 holds cols 0-1, T13 holds cols 2-3, T14 holds cols 4-5, T15 holds cols 6-7
  Row 4: T16 holds cols 0-1, T17 holds cols 2-3, T18 holds cols 4-5, T19 holds cols 6-7
  Row 5: T20 holds cols 0-1, T21 holds cols 2-3, T22 holds cols 4-5, T23 holds cols 6-7
  Row 6: T24 holds cols 0-1, T25 holds cols 2-3, T26 holds cols 4-5, T27 holds cols 6-7
  Row 7: T28 holds cols 0-1, T29 holds cols 2-3, T30 holds cols 4-5, T31 holds cols 6-7

Matrix rows 8-15 (lower half), registers c2 and c3:
  Row 8:  T0 holds cols 0-1, T1 holds cols 2-3, T2 holds cols 4-5, T3 holds cols 6-7
  Row 9:  T4 holds cols 0-1, T5 holds cols 2-3, T6 holds cols 4-5, T7 holds cols 6-7
  Row 10: T8 holds cols 0-1, T9 holds cols 2-3, T10 holds cols 4-5, T11 holds cols 6-7
  Row 11: T12 holds cols 0-1, T13 holds cols 2-3, T14 holds cols 4-5, T15 holds cols 6-7
  Row 12: T16 holds cols 0-1, T17 holds cols 2-3, T18 holds cols 4-5, T19 holds cols 6-7
  Row 13: T20 holds cols 0-1, T21 holds cols 2-3, T22 holds cols 4-5, T23 holds cols 6-7
  Row 14: T24 holds cols 0-1, T25 holds cols 2-3, T26 holds cols 4-5, T27 holds cols 6-7
  Row 15: T28 holds cols 0-1, T29 holds cols 2-3, T30 holds cols 4-5, T31 holds cols 6-7

REGISTER SEMANTICS PER THREAD
Each thread holds exactly 4 registers: c0, c1, c2, c3
  c0 = element at the lower column index of the thread's assigned column pair, upper 8 rows
  c1 = element at the higher column index of the thread's assigned column pair, upper 8 rows
  c2 = element at the lower column index of the thread's assigned column pair, lower 8 rows
  c3 = element at the higher column index of the thread's assigned column pair, lower 8 rows

GROUPING PATTERN SUMMARY
Threads T0-T3:   cover all 8 columns of matrix rows 0 and 8
Threads T4-T7:   cover all 8 columns of matrix rows 1 and 9
Threads T8-T11:  cover all 8 columns of matrix rows 2 and 10
Threads T12-T15: cover all 8 columns of matrix rows 3 and 11
Threads T16-T19: cover all 8 columns of matrix rows 4 and 12
Threads T20-T23: cover all 8 columns of matrix rows 5 and 13
Threads T24-T27: cover all 8 columns of matrix rows 6 and 14
Threads T28-T31: cover all 8 columns of matrix rows 7 and 15

Each thread covers exactly 2 consecutive columns (a pair) and 2 rows (one from rows 0-7 via c0/c1, one from rows 8-15 via c2/c3), yielding 4 elements total stored in 4 separate 32-bit .s32 registers.

MMA .m16n8k128 Fragment Layout for Accumulator Matrix C/D with .s32 Type

OVERVIEW
Matrix dimensions: M=16, N=8, K=128
Matrix role: Accumulator (C/D)
Data type: .s32
Each .s32 element occupies one full 32-bit register (no packing; one element per register)
Each thread holds 4 registers total: c0, c1, c2, c3

GRID STRUCTURE
The table has rows labeled R (matrix rows 0-15) and columns labeled C (matrix columns 0-7).
Columns are grouped in pairs: {0,1}, {2,3}, {4,5}, {6,7}.

TABLE LAYOUT (R\C = matrix row \ column group)

R\C     Cols 0-1          Cols 2-3          Cols 4-5          Cols 6-7
0       T0:  {c0, c1}     T1:  {c0, c1}     T2:  {c0, c1}     T3:  {c0, c1}
1       T4:  {c0, c1}     T5:  {c0, c1}     T6:  {c0, c1}     T7:  {c0, c1}
2       [continuation arrow indicating pattern continues through rows 2-6]
...
7       T28: {c0, c1}     T29: {c0, c1}     T30: {c0, c1}     T31: {c0, c1}
8       T0:  {c2, c3}     T1:  {c2, c3}     T2:  {c2, c3}     T3:  {c2, c3}
9       T4:  {c2, c3}     T5:  {c2, c3}     T6:  {c2, c3}     T7:  {c2, c3}
10      [continuation arrow indicating pattern continues through rows 10-14]
...
15      T28: {c2, c3}     T29: {c2, c3}     T30: {c2, c3}     T31: {c2, c3}

DETAILED THREAD-TO-ELEMENT MAPPING

Matrix rows 0-7 (upper half), registers c0 and c1:
  Row 0: T0 holds cols 0-1, T1 holds cols 2-3, T2 holds cols 4-5, T3 holds cols 6-7
  Row 1: T4 holds cols 0-1, T5 holds cols 2-3, T6 holds cols 4-5, T7 holds cols 6-7
  Row 2: T8 holds cols 0-1, T9 holds cols 2-3, T10 holds cols 4-5, T11 holds cols 6-7
  Row 3: T12 holds cols 0-1, T13 holds cols 2-3, T14 holds cols 4-5, T15 holds cols 6-7
  Row 4: T16 holds cols 0-1, T17 holds cols 2-3, T18 holds cols 4-5, T19 holds cols 6-7
  Row 5: T20 holds cols 0-1, T21 holds cols 2-3, T22 holds cols 4-5, T23 holds cols 6-7
  Row 6: T24 holds cols 0-1, T25 holds cols 2-3, T26 holds cols 4-5, T27 holds cols 6-7
  Row 7: T28 holds cols 0-1, T29 holds cols 2-3, T30 holds cols 4-5, T31 holds cols 6-7

Matrix rows 8-15 (lower half), registers c2 and c3:
  Row 8:  T0 holds cols 0-1, T1 holds cols 2-3, T2 holds cols 4-5, T3 holds cols 6-7
  Row 9:  T4 holds cols 0-1, T5 holds cols 2-3, T6 holds cols 4-5, T7 holds cols 6-7
  Row 10: T8 holds cols 0-1, T9 holds cols 2-3, T10 holds cols 4-5, T11 holds cols 6-7
  Row 11: T12 holds cols 0-1, T13 holds cols 2-3, T14 holds cols 4-5, T15 holds cols 6-7
  Row 12: T16 holds cols 0-1, T17 holds cols 2-3, T18 holds cols 4-5, T19 holds cols 6-7
  Row 13: T20 holds cols 0-1, T21 holds cols 2-3, T22 holds cols 4-5, T23 holds cols 6-7
  Row 14: T24 holds cols 0-1, T25 holds cols 2-3, T26 holds cols 4-5, T27 holds cols 6-7
  Row 15: T28 holds cols 0-1, T29 holds cols 2-3, T30 holds cols 4-5, T31 holds cols 6-7

REGISTER SEMANTICS PER THREAD
Each thread holds exactly 4 registers: c0, c1, c2, c3
  c0 = element at the lower column index of the thread's assigned column pair, upper 8 rows
  c1 = element at the higher column index of the thread's assigned column pair, upper 8 rows
  c2 = element at the lower column index of the thread's assigned column pair, lower 8 rows
  c3 = element at the higher column index of the thread's assigned column pair, lower 8 rows

GROUPING PATTERN SUMMARY
Threads T0-T3:   cover all 8 columns of matrix rows 0 and 8
Threads T4-T7:   cover all 8 columns of matrix rows 1 and 9
Threads T8-T11:  cover all 8 columns of matrix rows 2 and 10
Threads T12-T15: cover all 8 columns of matrix rows 3 and 11
Threads T16-T19: cover all 8 columns of matrix rows 4 and 12
Threads T20-T23: cover all 8 columns of matrix rows 5 and 13
Threads T24-T27: cover all 8 columns of matrix rows 6 and 14
Threads T28-T31: cover all 8 columns of matrix rows 7 and 15

Each thread covers exactly 2 consecutive columns (a pair) and 2 rows (one from rows 0-7 via c0/c1, one from rows 8-15 via c2/c3), yielding 4 elements total stored in 4 separate 32-bit .s32 registers.


  Figure 99 MMA .m16n8k128 fragment layout for accumulator matrix C/D with `.s32` type.

  The row and column of a matrix fragment can be computed as:

  ```
  groupID           = %laneid >> 2
  threadID_in_group = %laneid % 4

  row =      groupID                           for ci where i <  2
            groupID + 8                        for ci where i >= 2

  col =  (threadID_in_group * 2) + (i & 0x1)    for ci where i = {0, 1, 2, 3}
  ```

##### 9.7.14.5.13. [Matrix Fragments for `mma.m16n8k256`](#warp-level-matrix-fragment-mma-168256)

A warp executing `mma.m16n8k256` will compute an MMA operation of shape `.m16n8k256`.

Elements of the matrix are distributed across the threads in a warp so each thread of the warp holds
a fragment of the matrix.

* Multiplicand A:

  | .atype | Fragment | Elements (low to high) |
  | --- | --- | --- |
  | `.b1` | A vector expression containing four `.b32` registers, with each register containing thirty two `.b1` elements from the matrix A. | a0, a1, …, a126, a127 |

  The layout of the fragments held by different threads is shown in [Figure 100](#mma-168256-a).

  



[Figure: mma-168256-A]
MMA .m16n8k256 Fragment Layout for Matrix A with .b1 type

1. MATRIX DIMENSIONS AND IDENTITY
   Instruction: MMA .m16n8k256
   Matrix: A (input matrix)
   Data type: .b1 (1-bit binary)
   Dimensions: M=16, N=8, K=256

2. GRID STRUCTURE
   Rows (R): 0 through 15 — representing 16 rows of the matrix A tile
   Columns (C): 0 through 255 — representing 256 columns of the K dimension
   The table is divided into two halves horizontally:
     Left half: columns 0..127 (C=0 to 127)
     Right half: columns 128..255 (C=128 to 255)
   The table is divided into two halves vertically:
     Upper half: rows 0..7 (R=0 to 7)
     Lower half: rows 8..15 (R=8 to 15)

3. COLUMN GROUPINGS (K dimension bands)
   Left half, columns 0..127 is subdivided into four bands:
     C=0..31, C=32..63, C=64..95, C=96..127
   Right half, columns 128..255 is subdivided into four bands:
     C=128..159, C=160..191, C=192..223, C=224..255

4. THREAD-TO-ELEMENT MAPPING (detailed per cell)

   UPPER-LEFT QUADRANT (Rows 0..7, Columns 0..127):
   Each thread holds elements a0 through a31 (32 elements) for columns 0..31 through 96..127 combined.
   The notation "{a0, a1, .., a31}" means 32 consecutive 1-bit elements packed into registers.

   Row 0:
     C=0..31:   T0:{a0, a1, .., a31}
     C=32..63:  T1:{a0, a1, .., a31}
     C=64..95:  T2:{a0, a1, .., a31}
     C=96..127: T3:{a0, a1, .., a31}

   Row 1:
     C=0..31:   T4:{a0, a1, .., a31}
     C=32..63:  T5:{a0, a1, .., a31}
     C=64..95:  T6:{a0, a1, .., a31}
     C=96..127: T7:{a0, a1, .., a31}

   Rows 2..6: continuation pattern indicated by arrows (same structure, threads T8..T27)

   Row 7:
     C=0..31:   T28:{a0, a1, .., a31}
     C=32..63:  T29:{a0, a1, .., a31}
     C=64..95:  T30:{a0, a1, .., a31}
     C=96..127: T31:{a0, a1, .., a31}

   UPPER-RIGHT QUADRANT (Rows 0..7, Columns 128..255):
   Each thread holds elements a64 through a95 (32 elements) for columns 128..255.

   Row 0:
     C=128..159: T0:{a64, .., a95}
     C=160..191: T1:{a64, .., a95}
     C=192..223: T2:{a64, .., a95}
     C=224..255: T3:{a64, .., a95}

   Row 1:
     C=128..159: T4:{a64, .., a95}
     C=160..191: T5:{a64, .., a95}
     C=192..223: T6:{a64, .., a95}
     C=224..255: T7:{a64, .., a95}

   Rows 2..6: continuation pattern (threads T8..T27)

   Row 7:
     C=128..159: T28:{a64, .., a95}
     C=160..191: T29:{a64, .., a95}
     C=192..223: T30:{a64, .., a95}
     C=224..255: T31:{a64, .., a95}

   LOWER-LEFT QUADRANT (Rows 8..15, Columns 0..127):
   Each thread holds elements a32 through a63 (32 elements).

   Row 8:
     C=0..31:   T0:{a32, .., a63}
     C=32..63:  T1:{a32, .., a63}
     C=64..95:  T2:{a32, .., a63}
     C=96..127: T3:{a32, .., a63}

   Row 9:
     C=0..31:   T4:{a32, .., a63}
     C=32..63:  T5:{a32, .., a63}
     C=64..95:  T6:{a32, .., a63}
     C=96..127: T7:{a32, .., a63}

   Rows 10..14: continuation pattern (threads T8..T27)

   Row 15:
     C=0..31:   T28:{a32, .., a63}
     C=32..63:  T29:{a32, .., a63}
     C=64..95:  T30:{a32, .., a63}
     C=96..127: T31:{a32, .., a63}

   LOWER-RIGHT QUADRANT (Rows 8..15, Columns 128..255):
   Each thread holds elements a96 through a127 (32 elements).

   Row 8:
     C=128..159: T0:{a96, .., a127}
     C=160..191: T1:{a96, .., a127}
     C=192..223: T2:{a96, .., a127}
     C=224..255: T3:{a96, .., a127}

   Row 9:
     C=128..159: T4:{a96, .., a127}
     C=160..191: T5:{a96, .., a127}
     C=192..223: T6:{a96, .., a127}
     C=224..255: T7:{a96, .., a127}

   Rows 10..14: continuation pattern (threads T8..T27)

   Row 15:
     C=128..159: T28:{a96, .., a127}
     C=160..191: T29:{a96, .., a127}
     C=192..223: T30:{a96, .., a127}
     C=224..255: T31:{a96, .., a127}

5. GROUPING PATTERNS
   Rows 0..7 (upper half): All 32 threads (T0..T31) each cover 4 consecutive 32-column bands across K=0..127 and K=128..255.
   Rows 8..15 (lower half): Same 32 threads (T0..T31) cover the second set of element indices (a32..a63 and a96..a127).
   Within each row group of 8 rows, threads T0..T3 cover row 0, T4..T7 cover row 1, ..., T28..T31 cover row 7.
   Each thread covers exactly one matrix row and one 32-column K-band per quadrant.

6. ELEMENT PACKING WITHIN REGISTERS
   Data type is .b1 (1-bit binary).
   Each thread holds 32 one-bit elements per fragment group (e.g., a0..a31, a32..a63, a64..a95, a96..a127).
   32 one-bit elements pack into a single 32-bit register (one bit per element).
   Each thread therefore holds 4 groups of 32 bits = 4 registers total for the full K=256 dimension:
     Register group 1: a0..a31 (columns 0..31 of K, rows 0..7)
     Register group 2: a32..a63 (columns 32..63 of K, rows 8..15 mapping)
     Register group 3: a64..a95 (columns 128..159 of K, rows 0..7)
     Register group 4: a96..a127 (columns 160..191 of K, rows 8..15 mapping)

7. LANE ID NOTATION
   The figure footer states: %laneid:{fragments}
   This confirms that the thread index corresponds directly to the lane ID within the warp (lanes 0..31).

MMA .m16n8k256 Fragment Layout for Matrix A with .b1 type

1. MATRIX DIMENSIONS AND IDENTITY
   Instruction: MMA .m16n8k256
   Matrix: A (input matrix)
   Data type: .b1 (1-bit binary)
   Dimensions: M=16, N=8, K=256

2. GRID STRUCTURE
   Rows (R): 0 through 15 — representing 16 rows of the matrix A tile
   Columns (C): 0 through 255 — representing 256 columns of the K dimension
   The table is divided into two halves horizontally:
     Left half: columns 0..127 (C=0 to 127)
     Right half: columns 128..255 (C=128 to 255)
   The table is divided into two halves vertically:
     Upper half: rows 0..7 (R=0 to 7)
     Lower half: rows 8..15 (R=8 to 15)

3. COLUMN GROUPINGS (K dimension bands)
   Left half, columns 0..127 is subdivided into four bands:
     C=0..31, C=32..63, C=64..95, C=96..127
   Right half, columns 128..255 is subdivided into four bands:
     C=128..159, C=160..191, C=192..223, C=224..255

4. THREAD-TO-ELEMENT MAPPING (detailed per cell)

   UPPER-LEFT QUADRANT (Rows 0..7, Columns 0..127):
   Each thread holds elements a0 through a31 (32 elements) for columns 0..31 through 96..127 combined.
   The notation "{a0, a1, .., a31}" means 32 consecutive 1-bit elements packed into registers.

   Row 0:
     C=0..31:   T0:{a0, a1, .., a31}
     C=32..63:  T1:{a0, a1, .., a31}
     C=64..95:  T2:{a0, a1, .., a31}
     C=96..127: T3:{a0, a1, .., a31}

   Row 1:
     C=0..31:   T4:{a0, a1, .., a31}
     C=32..63:  T5:{a0, a1, .., a31}
     C=64..95:  T6:{a0, a1, .., a31}
     C=96..127: T7:{a0, a1, .., a31}

   Rows 2..6: continuation pattern indicated by arrows (same structure, threads T8..T27)

   Row 7:
     C=0..31:   T28:{a0, a1, .., a31}
     C=32..63:  T29:{a0, a1, .., a31}
     C=64..95:  T30:{a0, a1, .., a31}
     C=96..127: T31:{a0, a1, .., a31}

   UPPER-RIGHT QUADRANT (Rows 0..7, Columns 128..255):
   Each thread holds elements a64 through a95 (32 elements) for columns 128..255.

   Row 0:
     C=128..159: T0:{a64, .., a95}
     C=160..191: T1:{a64, .., a95}
     C=192..223: T2:{a64, .., a95}
     C=224..255: T3:{a64, .., a95}

   Row 1:
     C=128..159: T4:{a64, .., a95}
     C=160..191: T5:{a64, .., a95}
     C=192..223: T6:{a64, .., a95}
     C=224..255: T7:{a64, .., a95}

   Rows 2..6: continuation pattern (threads T8..T27)

   Row 7:
     C=128..159: T28:{a64, .., a95}
     C=160..191: T29:{a64, .., a95}
     C=192..223: T30:{a64, .., a95}
     C=224..255: T31:{a64, .., a95}

   LOWER-LEFT QUADRANT (Rows 8..15, Columns 0..127):
   Each thread holds elements a32 through a63 (32 elements).

   Row 8:
     C=0..31:   T0:{a32, .., a63}
     C=32..63:  T1:{a32, .., a63}
     C=64..95:  T2:{a32, .., a63}
     C=96..127: T3:{a32, .., a63}

   Row 9:
     C=0..31:   T4:{a32, .., a63}
     C=32..63:  T5:{a32, .., a63}
     C=64..95:  T6:{a32, .., a63}
     C=96..127: T7:{a32, .., a63}

   Rows 10..14: continuation pattern (threads T8..T27)

   Row 15:
     C=0..31:   T28:{a32, .., a63}
     C=32..63:  T29:{a32, .., a63}
     C=64..95:  T30:{a32, .., a63}
     C=96..127: T31:{a32, .., a63}

   LOWER-RIGHT QUADRANT (Rows 8..15, Columns 128..255):
   Each thread holds elements a96 through a127 (32 elements).

   Row 8:
     C=128..159: T0:{a96, .., a127}
     C=160..191: T1:{a96, .., a127}
     C=192..223: T2:{a96, .., a127}
     C=224..255: T3:{a96, .., a127}

   Row 9:
     C=128..159: T4:{a96, .., a127}
     C=160..191: T5:{a96, .., a127}
     C=192..223: T6:{a96, .., a127}
     C=224..255: T7:{a96, .., a127}

   Rows 10..14: continuation pattern (threads T8..T27)

   Row 15:
     C=128..159: T28:{a96, .., a127}
     C=160..191: T29:{a96, .., a127}
     C=192..223: T30:{a96, .., a127}
     C=224..255: T31:{a96, .., a127}

5. GROUPING PATTERNS
   Rows 0..7 (upper half): All 32 threads (T0..T31) each cover 4 consecutive 32-column bands across K=0..127 and K=128..255.
   Rows 8..15 (lower half): Same 32 threads (T0..T31) cover the second set of element indices (a32..a63 and a96..a127).
   Within each row group of 8 rows, threads T0..T3 cover row 0, T4..T7 cover row 1, ..., T28..T31 cover row 7.
   Each thread covers exactly one matrix row and one 32-column K-band per quadrant.

6. ELEMENT PACKING WITHIN REGISTERS
   Data type is .b1 (1-bit binary).
   Each thread holds 32 one-bit elements per fragment group (e.g., a0..a31, a32..a63, a64..a95, a96..a127).
   32 one-bit elements pack into a single 32-bit register (one bit per element).
   Each thread therefore holds 4 groups of 32 bits = 4 registers total for the full K=256 dimension:
     Register group 1: a0..a31 (columns 0..31 of K, rows 0..7)
     Register group 2: a32..a63 (columns 32..63 of K, rows 8..15 mapping)
     Register group 3: a64..a95 (columns 128..159 of K, rows 0..7)
     Register group 4: a96..a127 (columns 160..191 of K, rows 8..15 mapping)

7. LANE ID NOTATION
   The figure footer states: %laneid:{fragments}
   This confirms that the thread index corresponds directly to the lane ID within the warp (lanes 0..31).


  Figure 100 MMA .m16n8k256 fragment layout for matrix A with `.b1` type.

  The row and column of a matrix fragment can be computed as:

  ```
  groupID = %laneid >> 2
  threadID_in_group = %laneid % 4

  row =   groupID                                            for ai where 0 <= i < 32 || 64 <= i < 96
          groupID + 8                                        otherwise

  col =      (threadID_in_group * 32) + i                    for ai where i < 64
             (threadID_in_group * 32) + (i & 0x1F) + 128     for ai where i >= 64
  ```
* Multiplicand B:

  | .btype | Fragment | Elements (low to high) |
  | --- | --- | --- |
  | `.b1` | A vector expression containing two `.b32` registers, with each register containing thirty two `.b1` elements from the matrix B. | b0, b1, …, b62, b63 |

  The layout of the fragments held by different threads is shown in [Figure 101](#mma-168256-b-1) and
  [Figure 102](#mma-168256-b-2).

  



[Figure: mma-168256-B_1]
MMA .m16n8k256 Fragment Layout for Matrix B (rows 0-127), .b1 type

1. MATRIX DIMENSIONS AND IDENTITY
   Instruction: MMA .m16n8k256
   Matrix: B (input matrix)
   Data type: .b1 (1-bit binary / binary matrix)
   This figure covers rows 0 through 127 of matrix B
   (There are additional rows 128-255 not shown in this figure)

2. GRID STRUCTURE
   Rows (outer): Groups of matrix rows, labeled 0..31, 32..63, 64..95, 96..127
   Columns: 0, 1, 2, .., 7 (8 columns total, indices 0 through 7)
   Each cell contains one thread identifier and its register contents

3. THREAD-TO-ELEMENT MAPPING

   Row group 0..31 (matrix rows 0-31):
     Col 0: T0  holds {b0, b1, .., b31}
     Col 1: T4  holds {b0, b1, .., b31}
     Col 2: (implied, follows pattern)
     ..
     Col 7: T28 holds {b0, b1, .., b31}

   Row group 32..63 (matrix rows 32-63):
     Col 0: T1  holds {b0, b1, .., b31}
     Col 1: T5  holds {b0, b1, .., b31}
     Col 2: (implied, follows pattern)
     ..
     Col 7: T29 holds {b0, b1, .., b31}

   Row group 64..95 (matrix rows 64-95):
     Col 0: T2  holds {b0, b1, .., b31}
     Col 1: T6  holds {b0, b1, .., b31}
     Col 2: (implied, follows pattern)
     ..
     Col 7: T30 holds {b0, b1, .., b31}

   Row group 96..127 (matrix rows 96-127):
     Col 0: T3  holds {b0, b1, .., b31}
     Col 1: T7  holds {b0, b1, .., b31}
     Col 2: (implied, follows pattern)
     ..
     Col 7: T31 holds {b0, b1, .., b31}

4. REGISTER ELEMENT DETAILS
   Each thread holds registers labeled b0 through b31 (32 register elements total per thread per column position).
   The notation {b0, b1, .., b31} indicates 32 register values per thread.

5. GROUPING PATTERNS
   Thread assignment follows this rule:
     - Rows 0-31:   threads T0, T4, T8, T12, T16, T20, T24, T28 (for cols 0-7 respectively)
     - Rows 32-63:  threads T1, T5, T9, T13, T17, T21, T25, T29 (for cols 0-7 respectively)
     - Rows 64-95:  threads T2, T6, T10, T14, T18, T22, T26, T30 (for cols 0-7 respectively)
     - Rows 96-127: threads T3, T7, T11, T15, T19, T23, T27, T31 (for cols 0-7 respectively)

   Pattern: Within each column group of 4 threads (e.g., T0,T1,T2,T3), each thread covers a consecutive block of 32 rows. Across columns, the thread index increments by 4 (T0, T4, T8... for row group 0-31).

6. PACKING WITHIN REGISTERS
   Data type is .b1 (1-bit). Each register element (b0..b31) is a 32-bit register holding 32 packed 1-bit values. Thus each thread holds 32 registers x 32 bits = 1024 bits of matrix B data per column assignment.

7. ANNOTATION
   The figure label at the bottom reads: %laneid:{fragments}
   indicating that the thread lane ID determines which fragments (register sets) are assigned to each thread.
   An arrow in the figure spans from column 2, row group 96-127 area up to column 7, row group 0-31 area, indicating the continuation/wrap of the column indexing pattern from col 2 through col 7.

[Figure: mma-168256-B_2]
MMA .m16n8k256 Fragment Layout for Rows 128-255 of Matrix B with .b1 type

1. MATRIX DIMENSIONS AND IDENTITY
   Instruction: MMA .m16n8k256
   Matrix: B (input matrix)
   Data type: .b1 (1-bit binary)
   This figure covers rows 128-255 of matrix B (the second half of a 256-row K dimension)

2. GRID STRUCTURE
   Rows (vertical axis): Matrix row ranges covered by thread groups
     - Rows 128-159
     - Rows 160-191
     - Rows 192-223
     - Rows 224-255
   Columns (horizontal axis): Register/fragment columns 0, 1, 2, .., 7 (8 columns total)

3. REGISTER ELEMENT CONTENTS
   Each thread holds elements b32, b33, .., b63 (32 elements packed into registers, indices 32 through 63)
   This represents a set of 32 register bits (fragments b32 through b63) per thread per column position.

4. THREAD-TO-ELEMENT MAPPING (complete)

   Row range 128-159:
     Column 0: T0  holds {b32, b33, .., b63}
     Column 1: T4  holds {b32, b33, .., b63}
     Column 2: (implied continuation)
     ..
     Column 7: T28 holds {b32, b33, .., b63}

   Row range 160-191:
     Column 0: T1  holds {b32, b33, .., b63}
     Column 1: T5  holds {b32, b33, .., b63}
     Column 2: (implied continuation)
     ..
     Column 7: T29 holds {b32, b33, .., b63}

   Row range 192-223:
     Column 0: T2  holds {b32, b33, .., b63}
     Column 1: T6  holds {b32, b33, .., b63}
     Column 2: (implied continuation)
     ..
     Column 7: T30 holds {b32, b33, .., b63}

   Row range 224-255:
     Column 0: T3  holds {b32, b33, .., b63}
     Column 1: T7  holds {b32, b33, .., b63}
     Column 2: (implied continuation)
     ..
     Column 7: T31 holds {b32, b33, .., b63}

5. GROUPING PATTERNS
   - Threads T0-T3 cover row groups 128-159, 160-191, 192-223, 224-255 respectively, all at column 0
   - Threads T4-T7 cover the same four row groups respectively at column 1
   - Threads T28-T31 cover the same four row groups respectively at column 7
   - The pattern increments thread index by 4 per column step: column c uses threads T(4c), T(4c+1), T(4c+2), T(4c+3) for the four row bands
   - Total threads used: T0 through T31 (32 threads, one full warp)

6. PACKING WITHIN REGISTERS
   - Each thread holds elements labeled b32 through b63, which are 32 one-bit values
   - These 32 bits are packed into a single 32-bit register (one 32-bit register holds 32 individual .b1 elements)
   - The notation {b32, b33, .., b63} indicates 32 consecutive bit elements packed into the register fragment
   - This is the second set of 32 bits (b32-b63) corresponding to rows 128-255, complementing the first set (b0-b31) for rows 0-127 shown in a companion figure

7. ADDRESSING
   - Index label at bottom: %laneid:{fragments}
   - The lane ID (0-31) determines which thread holds which row band and column fragment
   - Lane ID mod 4 determines the row band (0->128-159, 1->160-191, 2->192-223, 3->224-255)
   - Lane ID div 4 determines the column index (0 through 7)

1. MATRIX DIMENSIONS AND IDENTITY
   Instruction: MMA .m16n8k256

     - Rows 128-159

     - Rows 224-255

3. REGISTER ELEMENT CONTENTS
   Each thread holds elements b32, b33, .., b63 (32 elements packed into registers, indices 32 through 63)
   This represents a set of 32 register bits (fragments b32 through b63) per thread per column position.

4. THREAD-TO-ELEMENT MAPPING (complete)

   Row range 128-159:
     Column 0: T0  holds {b32, b33, .., b63}
     Column 1: T4  holds {b32, b33, .., b63}
     Column 2: (implied continuation)

     Column 0: T1  holds {b32, b33, .., b63}

   Row range 192-223:
     Column 0: T2  holds {b32, b33, .., b63}
     Column 1: T6  holds {b32, b33, .., b63}

5. GROUPING PATTERNS
   - Threads T0-T3 cover row groups 128-159, 160-191, 192-223, 224-255 respectively, all at column 0
   - Threads T4-T7 cover the same four row groups respectively at column 1
   - Threads T28-T31 cover the same four row groups respectively at column 7
   - The pattern increments thread index by 4 per column step: column c uses threads T(4c), T(4c+1), T(4c+2), T(4c+3) for the four row bands
   - Total threads used: T0 through T31 (32 threads, one full warp)

6. PACKING WITHIN REGISTERS
   - Each thread holds elements labeled b32 through b63, which are 32 one-bit values
   - These 32 bits are packed into a single 32-bit register (one 32-bit register holds 32 individual .b1 elements)
   - The notation {b32, b33, .., b63} indicates 32 consecutive bit elements packed into the register fragment
   - This is the second set of 32 bits (b32-b63) corresponding to rows 128-255, complementing the first set (b0-b31) for rows 0-127 shown in a companion figure

   - Index label at bottom: %laneid:{fragments}
   - The lane ID (0-31) determines which thread holds which row band and column fragment
   - Lane ID mod 4 determines the row band (0->128-159, 1->160-191, 2->192-223, 3->224-255)
   - Lane ID div 4 determines the column index (0 through 7)


  Figure 102 MMA .m16n8k256 fragment layout for rows 128–255 of matrix B with `.b1` type.

  The row and column of a matrix fragment can be computed as:

  ```
  groupID           = %laneid >> 2
  threadID_in_group = %laneid % 4

  row =      (threadID_in_group * 32) + (i & 0x1F)             for bi where i < 32
             (threadID_in_group * 32) + (i & 0x1F) + 128       for bi where i >= 32

  col =      groupID
  ```
* Accumulators (C or D):

  | .ctype / .dtype | Fragment | Elements (low to high) |
  | --- | --- | --- |
  | `.s32` | A vector expression containing four `.s32` registers, containing four `.s32` elements from the matrix C (or D). | c0, c1, c2, c3 |

  The layout of the fragments held by different threads is shown in [Figure 103](#mma-168256-c).

  



[Figure: mma-168256-C]
MMA .m16n8k256 Fragment Layout for Accumulator Matrix C/D with .s32 Type

OVERVIEW
Matrix dimensions: M=16, N=8, K=256
Matrix role: Accumulator (C/D)
Data type: .s32 (32-bit signed integer)
Each .s32 element occupies one full 32-bit register (no packing)
Total registers per thread: 4 (c0, c1, c2, c3)

GRID STRUCTURE
The table uses Row (R) and Column (C) axes.
Columns 0-7 represent the N dimension (8 columns total), grouped in pairs: {0,1}, {2,3}, {4,5}, {6,7}
Rows 0-15 represent the M dimension (16 rows total)

THREAD ASSIGNMENT TABLE

Row 0 (M=0):
  Columns 0-1: T0 holds {c0, c1}
  Columns 2-3: T1 holds {c0, c1}
  Columns 4-5: T2 holds {c0, c1}
  Columns 6-7: T3 holds {c0, c1}

Row 1 (M=1):
  Columns 0-1: T4 holds {c0, c1}
  Columns 2-3: T5 holds {c0, c1}
  Columns 4-5: T6 holds {c0, c1}
  Columns 6-7: T7 holds {c0, c1}

Rows 2-6 (M=2 through M=6):
  Pattern continues with threads T8 through T27 following the same structure (indicated by arrows showing continuation of the pattern from row 2 onward and back)

Row 7 (M=7):
  Columns 0-1: T28 holds {c0, c1}
  Columns 2-3: T29 holds {c0, c1}
  Columns 4-5: T30 holds {c0, c1}
  Columns 6-7: T31 holds {c0, c1}

Row 8 (M=8):
  Columns 0-1: T0 holds {c2, c3}
  Columns 2-3: T1 holds {c2, c3}
  Columns 4-5: T2 holds {c2, c3}
  Columns 6-7: T3 holds {c2, c3}

Row 9 (M=9):
  Columns 0-1: T4 holds {c2, c3}
  Columns 2-3: T5 holds {c2, c3}
  Columns 4-5: T6 holds {c2, c3}
  Columns 6-7: T7 holds {c2, c3}

Rows 10-14 (M=10 through M=14):
  Pattern continues with threads T8 through T27 following the same structure (indicated by arrows)

Row 15 (M=15):
  Columns 0-1: T28 holds {c2, c3}
  Columns 2-3: T29 holds {c2, c3}
  Columns 4-5: T30 holds {c2, c3}
  Columns 6-7: T31 holds {c2, c3}

GROUPING PATTERN SUMMARY
Threads T0-T31 cover all 32 threads in a warp.
Each thread covers 2 matrix rows and all 8 columns via 4 registers.
Rows 0-7 use registers c0 and c1.
Rows 8-15 use registers c2 and c3.
Within each row group of 8 rows, threads T0-T3 handle row 0 (and row 8), T4-T7 handle row 1 (and row 9), ..., T28-T31 handle row 7 (and row 15).
Each thread covers 2 consecutive columns: T0,T4,...,T28 cover cols 0-1; T1,T5,...,T29 cover cols 2-3; T2,T6,...,T30 cover cols 4-5; T3,T7,...,T31 cover cols 6-7.

REGISTER ELEMENT DETAILS
c0: element at (row_low, col_pair_left) — one .s32 value
c1: element at (row_low, col_pair_right) — one .s32 value
c2: element at (row_high, col_pair_left) — one .s32 value
c3: element at (row_high, col_pair_right) — one .s32 value
Each register holds exactly one .s32 value (no packing).

MMA .m16n8k256 Fragment Layout for Accumulator Matrix C/D with .s32 Type

OVERVIEW
Matrix dimensions: M=16, N=8, K=256
Matrix role: Accumulator (C/D)
Data type: .s32 (32-bit signed integer)
Each .s32 element occupies one full 32-bit register (no packing)
Total registers per thread: 4 (c0, c1, c2, c3)

GRID STRUCTURE
The table uses Row (R) and Column (C) axes.
Columns 0-7 represent the N dimension (8 columns total), grouped in pairs: {0,1}, {2,3}, {4,5}, {6,7}
Rows 0-15 represent the M dimension (16 rows total)

THREAD ASSIGNMENT TABLE

Row 0 (M=0):
  Columns 0-1: T0 holds {c0, c1}
  Columns 2-3: T1 holds {c0, c1}
  Columns 4-5: T2 holds {c0, c1}
  Columns 6-7: T3 holds {c0, c1}

Row 1 (M=1):

  Columns 2-3: T5 holds {c0, c1}
  Columns 4-5: T6 holds {c0, c1}
  Columns 6-7: T7 holds {c0, c1}

  Pattern continues with threads T8 through T27 following the same structure (indicated by arrows showing continuation of the pattern from row 2 onward and back)

Row 7 (M=7):
  Columns 0-1: T28 holds {c0, c1}
  Columns 2-3: T29 holds {c0, c1}
  Columns 4-5: T30 holds {c0, c1}
  Columns 6-7: T31 holds {c0, c1}

Row 8 (M=8):
  Columns 0-1: T0 holds {c2, c3}
  Columns 2-3: T1 holds {c2, c3}
  Columns 4-5: T2 holds {c2, c3}
  Columns 6-7: T3 holds {c2, c3}

Row 9 (M=9):
  Columns 0-1: T4 holds {c2, c3}
  Columns 2-3: T5 holds {c2, c3}
  Columns 4-5: T6 holds {c2, c3}
  Columns 6-7: T7 holds {c2, c3}

Rows 10-14 (M=10 through M=14):

Row 15 (M=15):
  Columns 0-1: T28 holds {c2, c3}
  Columns 2-3: T29 holds {c2, c3}
  Columns 4-5: T30 holds {c2, c3}
  Columns 6-7: T31 holds {c2, c3}

GROUPING PATTERN SUMMARY
Threads T0-T31 cover all 32 threads in a warp.
Each thread covers 2 matrix rows and all 8 columns via 4 registers.
Rows 0-7 use registers c0 and c1.
Rows 8-15 use registers c2 and c3.
Within each row group of 8 rows, threads T0-T3 handle row 0 (and row 8), T4-T7 handle row 1 (and row 9), ..., T28-T31 handle row 7 (and row 15).
Each thread covers 2 consecutive columns: T0,T4,...,T28 cover cols 0-1; T1,T5,...,T29 cover cols 2-3; T2,T6,...,T30 cover cols 4-5; T3,T7,...,T31 cover cols 6-7.

REGISTER ELEMENT DETAILS
c0: element at (row_low, col_pair_left) — one .s32 value
c1: element at (row_low, col_pair_right) — one .s32 value
c2: element at (row_high, col_pair_left) — one .s32 value
c3: element at (row_high, col_pair_right) — one .s32 value
Each register holds exactly one .s32 value (no packing).


  Figure 103 MMA .m16n8k256 fragment layout for accumulator matrix C/D with `.s32` type.

  The row and column of a matrix fragment can be computed as:

  ```
  groupID           = %laneid >> 2
  threadID_in_group = %laneid % 4

  row =        groupID                         for ci where i < 2
             groupID + 8                       for ci where i >= 2

  col =  (threadID_in_group * 2) + (i & 0x1)    for ci where i = {0, 1, 2, 3}
  ```

##### 9.7.14.5.14. [Multiply-and-Accumulate Instruction: `mma`](#warp-level-matrix-instructions-mma)

`mma`

Perform matrix multiply-and-accumulate operation

Syntax

Half precision floating point type:

```
mma.sync.aligned.m8n8k4.alayout.blayout.dtype.f16.f16.ctype  d, a, b, c;
mma.sync.aligned.m16n8k8.row.col.dtype.f16.f16.ctype  d, a, b, c;
mma.sync.aligned.m16n8k16.row.col.dtype.f16.f16.ctype d, a, b, c;

.alayout = {.row, .col};
.blayout = {.row, .col};
.ctype   = {.f16, .f32};
.dtype   = {.f16, .f32};
```

Alternate floating point type:

```
mma.sync.aligned.m16n8k4.row.col.f32.tf32.tf32.f32        d, a, b, c;
mma.sync.aligned.m16n8k8.row.col.f32.atype.btype.f32      d, a, b, c;
mma.sync.aligned.m16n8k16.row.col.f32.bf16.bf16.f32       d, a, b, c;
mma.sync.aligned.shape.row.col.dtype.f8type.f8type.ctype  d, a, b, c;
mma.sync.aligned.m16n8k32.row.col.kind.dtype.f8f6f4type.f8f6f4type.ctype d, a, b, c;

.atype      = {.bf16, .tf32};
.btype      = {.bf16, .tf32};
.f8type     = {.e4m3, .e5m2};
.f8f6f4type = {.e4m3, .e5m2, .e3m2, .e2m3, .e2m1};
.ctype      = {.f16, .f32};
.dtype      = {.f16, .f32};
.shape      = {.m16n8k16, .m16n8k32};
.kind       = {.kind::f8f6f4};
```

Alternate floating point type with block scaling:

```
mma.sync.aligned.m16n8k64.row.col.kind.block_scale{.scale_vec_size}.f32.e2m1.e2m1.f32.stype d, a, b, c, scale-a-data, {byte-id-a, thread-id-a}, scale-b-data, {byte-id-b, thread-id-b};

.kind           = {.kind::mxf4};
.scale_vec_size = {.scale_vec::2X};
.stype          = {.ue8m0};

mma.sync.aligned.m16n8k64.row.col.kind.block_scale.scale_vec_size.f32.e2m1.e2m1.f32.stype d, a, b, c, scale-a-data, {byte-id-a, thread-id-a}, scale-b-data, {byte-id-b, thread-id-b};

.kind           = {.kind::mxf4nvf4};
.scale_vec_size = {.scale_vec::2X, .scale_vec::4X};
.stype          = {.ue8m0, .ue4m3};

mma.sync.aligned.m16n8k32.row.col.kind.block_scale{.scale_vec_size}.f32.f8f6f4type.f8f6f4type.f32.stype d, a, b, c, scale-a-data, {byte-id-a, thread-id-a}, scale-b-data, {byte-id-b, thread-id-b};

.kind           = {.kind::mxf8f6f4};
.scale_vec_size = {.scale_vec::1X};
.f8f6f4type     = {.e4m3, .e5m2, .e3m2, .e2m3, .e2m1};
.stype          = {.ue8m0};
```

Double precision floating point type:

```
mma.sync.aligned.shape.row.col.f64.f64.f64.f64 d, a, b, c;

.shape   = {.m8n84, .m16n8k4, .m16n8k8, .m16n8k16};
```

Integer type:

```
mma.sync.aligned.shape.row.col{.satfinite}.s32.atype.btype.s32 d, a, b, c;

.shape   = {.m8n8k16, .m16n8k16, .m16n8k32}
.atype   = {.u8, .s8};
.btype   = {.u8, .s8};

mma.sync.aligned.shape.row.col{.satfinite}.s32.atype.btype.s32 d, a, b, c;

.shape   = {.m8n8k32, .m16n8k32, .m16n8k64}
.atype   = {.u4, .s4};
.btype   = {.u4, .s4};
```

Single bit:

```
mma.sync.aligned.shape.row.col.s32.b1.b1.s32.bitOp.popc d, a, b, c;

.bitOp = {.xor, .and}
.shape = {.m8n8k128, .m16n8k128, .m16n8k256}
```

Description

Perform a `MxNxK` matrix multiply and accumulate operation, `D = A*B+C`, where the A matrix is
`MxK`, the B matrix is `KxN`, and the C and D matrices are `MxN`.

Qualifier `.block_scale` specifies that the matrices A and B are scaled with `scale_A` and
`scale_B` matrices respectively before performing the matrix multiply and accumulate operation
as specified in the section [Block Scaling for mma.sync](#warp-level-block-scaling). The data type
corresponding to each of the element within `scale_A` and `Scale_B` matrices is specified
by `.stype`. Qualifier `.scale_vec_size` specifies the number of columns of `scale_A` matrix
and number of rows in the matrix `scale_B`.

The valid combinations of `.kind`, `.stype` and `.scale_vec_size` are described in
[Table 36](#mma-scaling-kind-type-valid-combination). For `mma` with `.kind::mxf4` when the
qualifier `.scale_vec_size` is not specified, then it defaults to `2X`. In contrast, when
`.kind` is specified as `.kind::mxf8f6f4` then the qualifier `.scale_vec_size` defaults
to `1X`. However, for `.kind::mxf4nvf4`, it is mandatory to provide valid `.scale_vec_size`.

A warp executing `mma.sync.m8n8k4` instruction computes 4 matrix multiply and accumulate
operations. Rest of the `mma.sync` operations compute a single matrix mutliply and accumulate
operation per warp.
